title: PBI Monitor
subtitle: Desktop automation app for daily Power BI dashboard refresh control
shortDescription: A working desktop application for automating the daily control of Power BI dashboard refresh status, built as an internal productivity tool for dashboard monitoring workflows.
problem: Daily dashboard refresh checks are repetitive, easy to miss and inefficient when they depend on manual inspection. A business analytics team needs a reliable way to track whether important dashboards were updated and to document the check without turning it into a daily manual routine.
solution: Build a focused desktop monitoring tool that centralizes dashboard refresh control, gives the user a clear daily check workflow and can evolve toward shared synchronization, notifications and report/export features.
myRole: Identified a real internal workflow problem around daily Power BI dashboard update checks.
Designed the tool as a practical desktop product rather than a one-off script.
Built the monitoring workflow around usability, maintainability and future synchronization needs.
Prepared documentation and positioned the app for use in a business analytics / technical development context.

architecture.title: Desktop application layer
items: Focused workflow for daily dashboard status checks
Designed for business users rather than developers only

architecture.title: Monitoring workflow
items: Clear separation between routine checks and configuration

architecture.title: Product evolution path
items: Shared data synchronization through a server folder or database
Notifications when another user changes dashboard data

architecture.title: Documentation layer
items: Clear framing for Jira/task closure and team adoption

result: Created a practical working product from a real internal analytics process.
Moved the task from manual daily checking toward repeatable automation.
Shows ability to build useful business tooling, not only portfolio demos.
whatILearned: Small internal tools still need product thinking: users, workflow, state, documentation and adoption.
Automation is valuable when it removes repeated attention cost from business teams.
The next architectural step should be shared synchronization and controlled multi-user updates.
whyItMatters: PBI Monitor is the strongest product-style case in the portfolio because it solves a real workflow problem: daily monitoring of dashboard updates for a business analytics environment.

screenshots.caption: Placeholder for the desktop application screen and dashboard refresh monitoring workflow.

title: AI Telegram Assistant
subtitle: Commandless RAG assistant for company knowledge
shortDescription: A Telegram assistant that works without command menus and answers only from a controlled company knowledge base using retrieval and local LLM infrastructure.
problem: Internal company knowledge is usually scattered across documents, chats and informal explanations. A normal Telegram bot with command buttons feels limited, while a generic LLM can hallucinate or answer outside the approved knowledge base.
solution: Design a RAG-first Telegram assistant that receives natural-language questions, retrieves relevant chunks from a curated knowledge base, sends only grounded context to the model and refuses to invent missing information.
myRole: Designed the assistant concept and commandless user experience.
Planned the RAG pipeline, local LLM flow and knowledge-base boundaries.
Worked on backend architecture around Python, aiogram, ChromaDB, PostgreSQL and Ollama.

architecture.title: Storage layer
items: Separation between chat state and knowledge content

features: Answers grounded in company knowledge base
Refusal behavior when knowledge is missing
result: Demonstrates practical LLM integration beyond a simple chatbot wrapper.
Shows understanding of grounded answers, retrieval and local AI workflows.
Useful as a real internal knowledge assistant prototype.
whatILearned: RAG systems need strong boundaries, not just embeddings.
A good assistant UX can be commandless while still controlled.
Local LLM demos are valuable when privacy or offline capability matters.
whyItMatters: This project proves AI/LLM engineering skills: provider integration, retrieval, prompt grounding, backend design and honest answer behavior.

screenshots.caption: Placeholder for Telegram assistant flow and RAG pipeline screenshot.

title: FinCat
subtitle: Financial organizations catalog with roles and API architecture
shortDescription: A full-stack catalog of financial organizations with DRF backend, Nuxt 3 frontend, JWT authentication, admin/user roles, CRUD operations and Dockerized PostgreSQL setup.
problem: The task required a small internal service where users can view financial organizations and admins can manage them. Backend structure, readable code, auth, roles and API design were more important than visual effects.
solution: Build a clean full-stack application with Django REST Framework endpoints, JWT authentication, role-based permissions, Nuxt 3 UI, organization cards and Docker Compose for backend, frontend and database.
myRole: Implemented the backend data model and CRUD API.
Designed role separation for admin and user flows.
Built the Nuxt frontend for browsing organization cards and details.
Prepared Docker setup and project documentation.

architecture.title: Permissions
items: Admin can create, update and delete

features: BIN, name, logo and licenses display
result: Completed the core requirements of a realistic full-stack technical assignment.
Demonstrated backend-first thinking with usable frontend delivery.
Created a case that shows API design, auth, roles and deployment readiness.
whatILearned: Backend architecture should stay clear before UI polish.
Role-based permissions are a core product requirement, not a bonus.
Docker makes even small test projects easier to evaluate and reproduce.
whyItMatters: FinCat is the strongest proof of practical full-stack/backend competence: CRUD, auth, roles, API structure, Docker and finance-domain data modeling.

screenshots.caption: Placeholder for organization catalog and admin flow screenshots.

title: AbafarConvertor
subtitle: YouTube-to-MP3 converter with Node backend and Expo/React Native client
shortDescription: A utility app that converts YouTube links into MP3 files using a Node/Express backend and a mobile/web client built with Expo and React Native.
problem: A simple media utility still needs a clear client/server split: the user enters a link in the app, the backend retrieves video metadata, converts audio to MP3 and returns the file safely to the client.
solution: Create a backend service with endpoints for video info and MP3 download, connect it to an Expo/React Native client and use yt-dlp/FFmpeg-style tooling for conversion workflow orchestration.
myRole: Built the app concept around a direct link-to-MP3 conversion workflow.
Implemented a Node/Express backend for metadata retrieval and download handling.
Structured the client app with Expo/React Native dependencies and API communication.
Handled file creation, response download and cleanup flow on the backend side.

architecture.title: Client application
items: React Native / React Native Web support

architecture.title: Backend API
items: POST /download for MP3 conversion and download

architecture.title: Product direction
items: Clear separation between UI and conversion backend
Good base for queueing, progress states and desktop packaging

result: Demonstrates practical client/server integration for a real utility workflow.
Shows backend process orchestration beyond standard CRUD.
Useful as a small product case that can be improved with security, queues and packaging.
whatILearned: Utility apps need strong backend boundaries because file processing can become risky and resource-heavy.
Conversion workflows should eventually add validation, progress states, queueing and safer command execution.
A small app can still show API design, mobile client structure and process automation.
whyItMatters: AbafarConvertor adds a practical utility/product case to the portfolio: Node backend, media processing workflow, Expo client and API integration.

screenshots.caption: Placeholder for the link input screen and backend conversion workflow diagram.

title: Век живи — век учись
subtitle: Educational progression clicker and frontend/game design case
shortDescription: A React-based educational clicker with knowledge progression, upgrades, eras, random events, prestige mechanics and interactive UI effects.
problem: A graduation web project needed to be more memorable than a standard CRUD app while still demonstrating frontend structure, state, UI systems and product thinking.
solution: Create an educational clicker where academic progress becomes a game loop: users gain knowledge and XP, unlock upgrades, pass through academic eras, react to events and use prestige mechanics.
myRole: Designed the game loop and progression systems.
Built React components and UI structure.
Implemented eras, upgrades, events and prestige logic.
Worked on visual feedback, animation and theme direction.
result: Turned a simple frontend assignment into a more distinctive interactive product.
Demonstrated ability to design systems, not just screens.
Good foundation for future gamified learning experiments.
whatILearned: Game loops need clear feedback and progression pacing.
Frontend architecture matters even in playful projects.
Animations should support user understanding, not just decoration.
whyItMatters: This project shows creative frontend ability: state-driven UI, interaction design, animation, game mechanics and modular React thinking.

screenshots.caption: Placeholder for progression screen, upgrades and events.

title: CBDC Financial Analytics Research
subtitle: Digital currency, macroeconomic indicators and econometric analysis
shortDescription: Master research on Central Bank Digital Currency impact using OLS regression, macroeconomic indicators, econometric diagnostics and Digital Tenge context.
problem: CBDC is often discussed as a technology topic, but its macroeconomic effects require analytical framing: inflation, money velocity, credit supply, financial inclusion and central bank policy transmission.
solution: Analyze CBDC-related macroeconomic scenarios using financial analytics, OLS regression, diagnostic checks and scenario reasoning around cash replacement and Digital Tenge implications.
myRole: Defined the research framing around CBDC and macroeconomic indicators.
Prepared data analysis workflow and econometric modeling.
Interpreted results for inflation, credit and policy transmission context.
Presented the work as a finance + data analytics research case.
result: Created a clear research bridge between finance, policy and data analysis.
Demonstrated ability to use analytical methods, not only software tools.
Built a strong FinTech/data case for the portfolio.
whatILearned: Financial technology questions need both technical and economic reasoning.
Regression output matters only when the interpretation is honest and contextual.
CBDC is a strong domain bridge between finance, data and systems thinking.
whyItMatters: This case gives the portfolio domain depth: finance, CBDC, macroeconomics, econometrics and analytical communication.

screenshots.caption: Placeholder for regression output, charts and thesis visuals.
